LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND DENIED AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, DEFENDANT MET ITS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT IT WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE CONSTRUCTION CONTRACT BETWEEN MANIAR AND ROYO AND NEVER ASSUMED MANIAR'S CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE. AS TO THE SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE ROYO DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE AND RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHEN LABOR CEASED AND WHETHER THE LIEN WAS TIMELY RECORDED. Defendant Far East National Bank Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Philip G. Allingham of Allingham & Cuerva LLP (508.4500) for plaintiff and responding party; Andrew R. Neilson of Nixon Peabody (984.8281) and William E. Robinson of Best Best & Krieger (916.325.4000) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
Motion for Summary Judgment is denied and alternate Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action and denied as to the second, third and fourth causes of action. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, defendant met its burden to show that it was not a party to the construction contract between Maniar and Royo and never assumed Maniar's contractual obligations and plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue. As to the second and fourth causes of action, the Royo declaration is admissible and raises a triable issue when labor ceased and whether the lien was timely recorded. As to the third cause of action, defendant has not satisfied the burden of negating an element of plaintiff's claim. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
